LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, NOV-30-2021 HEARING HELD RE: PETITIONER LABORERS LOCAL 261'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD OF AUGUST 17, 2021. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH A MODIFICATION. MOTION GRANTED. MATTER REMANDED TO THE ARBITRATOR. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) PETITIONER TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award Of August 17, 2021
Off Calendar Nov-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CAREN P. SENCER, Esq. of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld (Tel: 510-337-1001) for petitioner Laborers Local 261;
Atty. ANDREW H. BAKER, Esq. of Beeson, Tayer & Bodine, APC (Tel: 510-625-9700) for respondent OPEIU Local 29.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER LABORERS LOCAL 261's Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award Of August 17, 2021.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, as follows:

Laborers Local 261's petition to vacate the arbitration award of August 17, 2021 is granted. As the award recites, the issues the arbitrator was to decide were limited to (1) whether petitioner "violate[d] the collective bargaining agreement" and (2) "[i]f so, what shall the remedy be?" However, the award found violations of "federal law." The arbitrator thus exceeded his powers and the award must be vacated. (CCP 1286.2(a)(4); Delta Lines, Inc. v. International Brotherhood of Teamsters (1977) 66 Cal.App.3d 960; see also California Dept. of Human Resources v. Service Employees Internat. Union, Local 1000 (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 1420; Board of Education v. Round Valley Teachers Assn. (1996) 13 Cal.4th 269; Stenzor v. Leon (1955) 130 Cal.App.2d 729; Screen Cartoonists Guild v. Walt Disney Productions (1946) 74 Cal.App.2d 414.)

Respondent's late-filed opposition claims that the arbitrator merely used "federal law to guide him in determining whether Local 261 violated the CBA[]." But the award's plain language explicitly purported to find violations of federal law.

Matter is remanded to the arbitrator.

Petitioner to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award Of August 17, 2021
Off Calendar Nov-02-2021 Continued To Nov-30-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
